regions.us-ports: Today winds at Charleston are WSW at 5 knots with partly cloudy skies and an 11% chance of rain, so port operations and shore excursions across the US Ports region including Charleston, Savannah, and Norfolk are proceeding smoothly without weather-related delays. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, NE winds will increase to 10-11 knots with overcast skies and a 27% rain chance on Day 1, which may affect shore excursions and embarkation activities in Charleston and nearby ports such as Savannah and Jacksonville. By Day 2, the 0% rain probability alongside sustained NE winds of 11 knots with continued overcast skies will allow for stable cruise operations in Bayonne, Manhattan, and Port Canaveral. Beyond 48 hours, expect an ESE wind at 10 knots with overcast conditions and a 5% rain chance, indicating improving conditions for vessels docking in Miami, Port Everglades, and Tampa Bay without rain impacts. Crew and passengers should monitor evolving winds and cloud cover for any operational adjustments during this period.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today in the Bahamas and Central Caribbean, winds are easterly at 4 knots with clear skies and zero rain chance, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation at Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, and Key West. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect a shift to ESE at 7 knots with clear skies and a 1% rain chance, maintaining excellent conditions for shore excursions in Berry Islands, Grand Cayman, and Ocho Rios. By the second day, SSE winds increase slightly to 8 knots with overcast skies and a 4% rain chance, which will be noticeable but not disruptive for port operations in Falmouth, Puerto Plata, and La Romana. Beyond 48 hours, winds will return easterly at 11 knots with clear skies and a 2% rain chance, indicating continued favorable navigation and port access for Santo Domingo and Samaná. No marine wind warnings or significant weather hazards are expected across these cruise ports during this period.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, an ENE wind at 12 knots and a 47% chance of drizzle may affect shore excursions and embarkation operations in San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Maarten, with partly cloudy skies and generally good visibility elsewhere. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, southeast winds near 9 knots combined with a 71% probability of rain showers are expected to impact port operations and outdoor activities in St. Croix, St. Kitts, and Antigua. By day three, east-southeast winds easing to 7 knots will accompany a 79% chance of rain showers, which is expected to affect San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Maarten again, likely causing delays in shore excursions and disembarkation. Passengers and crew should prepare for persistent wet conditions through this period while monitoring updates as winds remain moderate with no small craft advisories currently in effect.
regions.western-caribbean: Today in Cozumel, winds are steady from the east-southeast at 6 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero chance of rain, providing excellent conditions for embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds to increase slightly to 9 to 10 knots from the southeast and east-southeast with continued partly cloudy skies and minimal rain probabilities of 2% to 10%, which will keep port operations smooth in Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift to the east at 9 knots accompanied by drizzle and a 13% rain chance in Cozumel, signaling light moisture but no operational impact. All ports should monitor these light wind and sky condition changes for routine adjustments, but no disruptions to cruise schedules or excursions are anticipated.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today in Aruba, winds are easterly at 8 knots with overcast skies and only a 1% chance of rain, so port operations and shore excursions are running smoothly without weather interruptions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect easterly winds increasing to 12-15 knots with drizzle and a 22-33% rain chance that may affect shore excursions in Aruba, Curacao, and Bonaire. These conditions could cause minor delays in embarkation or disembarkation procedures at these ports due to light precipitation and reduced visibility. Beyond 48 hours, easterly winds will strengthen to 18 knots with a 35% chance of drizzle around Aruba and nearby ports, which may continue to affect outdoor activities and require operational adjustments. Cartagena is forecast to experience lighter winds and lower rain probability during this period, presenting more favorable conditions for cruise operations.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today, winds are steady from the east-southeast at 10 knots with partly cloudy skies and a 13% chance of rain, supporting normal port operations and shore excursions across Barbados, St. Lucia, and Grenada. Crew and passengers should monitor for possible wet conditions but no advisories or warnings are in effect.
regions.los-angeles: Today winds out of the west at 6 knots bring overcast skies to the Los Angeles and San Pedro ports, with a 28% chance of rain that may affect embarkation and shore excursions in Long Beach and Marina del Rey. Passengers and crew should plan excursions and port visits accordingly with this steady, dry marine environment.

regions.central-mediterranean: Today in Naples, winds are south at 5 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent chance of rain, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect southwest winds at 6 to 7 knots accompanied by fog on days one and three, with an overcast sky and west-southwest winds at 6 knots on day two; these conditions may reduce visibility in Naples, Civitavecchia, and nearby ports such as Rome’s gateway, potentially slowing tender operations. Marseille, Nice, Monaco, Genoa, La Spezia, Livorno, Sardinia, Corsica, Split, Dubrovnik, and Venice should monitor for similar fog development impacting morning arrivals or departures given the regional wind shifts. Beyond 48 hours, foggy conditions with southwest winds near 7 knots will persist in Naples, maintaining reduced visibility that could continue to challenge docking schedules and shore excursions in southern ports. Rain is not expected at any point, ensuring dry conditions for cruise activities throughout the Central Mediterranean.
